title: Container can't connect to VPN if I don't use `Network=pasta:[OPTIONS]`
body:
### Issue Description

I used to have a container that connects to a VPN (Wireguard). To achieve this, the container had to be attached to a custom network or have the following settings: `Network=pasta:--ipv4-only,-a,10.0.2.0,-n,24,-g,10.0.2.2,--dns-forward,10.0.2.3,-m,1500,--no-ndp,--no-dhcpv6,--no-dhcp`.
It's important for me to have that container attached to my custom network as it needs to communicate to other containers.
Since yesterday, this container does not connect to the VPN anymore if not with those `pasta` settings (but then the container can't communicate with other containers...)

From inside the container, I see the wg0 interface and the IPs private/public that are defined in the Wireguard config file.

At the beginning I thought that the last podman/passt updates were the issues but I rolled back my system and I have that issue since then.

Any idea?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Deploy custom network
2. Deploy [this container](https://hotio.dev/containers/qbittorrent/) and attach it to the custom network
3. Verify missing VPN connectivity

### Describe the results you received

Container can't connect to VPN if not with `Network=pasta:--ipv4-only,-a,10.0.2.0,-n,24,-g,10.0.2.2,--dns-forward,10.0.2.3,-m,1500,--no-ndp,--no-dhcpv6,--no-dhcp`.

### Describe the results you expected

Container should connect to VPN as it used to be

### podman info output

comments.body:
> * Deploy custom network
> 
> * Deploy [this container](https://hotio.dev/containers/qbittorrent/) and attach it to the custom network
> 
> * Verify missing VPN connectivity

This isn't particular helpful. We need to know exact ip address/routes on the host and container (or in case of using the custom networks inside `podman unshare --rootless-netns`). You didn't provide your wireguard configs either so it is impossible to replicate your VPN setup as well. What does connection to the VPN mean and where is the tunnel setup, on the host or in the container?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

@Luap99 thanks for your quick reply

The VPN tunnel is setup inside the container.
This is my wg0.conf file with concealed info where applicable:
```[Interface]
Address = 10.173.229.181/32
PrivateKey = REDACTED
MTU = 1320
DNS = 10.128.0.1

[Peer]
PublicKey = REDACTED
PresharedKey = REDACTED
Endpoint = REDACTED
AllowedIPs = 0.0.0.0/0
PersistentKeepalive = 15
```

Container uses VPN to connect to the internet and exposed a port to my home private network in order to access its WebUI.

How can I provide the exact ip address/routes on the host and container? And yes, container it's attached to a custom network at the moment so I guess I have to provide extra info from `podman unshare --rootless-netns`: which command do I have run to provide the required info?

Thanks you!

@Luap99 I tried to run `podman system reset`. Container started and connected to the VPN tunnel.
However, when I restarted other containers, I got back to the same issue: no connectivity to the VPN tunnel.

Run `ip addr; ip route` on the host and in `podman unshare --rootless-netns` and provide output. If you have public ips in there feel free to retract them.

If wireguard is setup in the container it sounds very much like a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26438 as it seems to describe a similar problem. Try the package capture from  https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/26438#issuecomment-2979570443 as well.

@Luap99 I think there's more: while checking something else, I noticed that also other containers in the same custom network don't have internet access regardless of using a VPN tunnel or not.
If I pull any container outside of this custom network, they work immediately and they can reach the internet.

Here's the required output:


```podman@host:~> podman unshare --rootless-netns
10.88.0.0/16 dev podman0 proto kernel scope link
10.88.0.0/16 dev podman0 proto kernel scope link src 10.88.0.1
10.89.0.0/24 dev podman1 proto kernel scope link src 10.89.0.1
```

I haven't changed anything in my quadlets definition, this issue appeared out of nowhere.

Thanks, that output does indeed explain why things are not working but not how we got there.

First, you seems to be running rootful containers as well and therefore have a podman0 interface on the host.
Second, pasta normally picks the default route interface when it is started to copy the ip addr/routes from the host into the private namespace. For some reason it seemed to have picked podman0 instead of your actual host interface eno1.

Can you provide the output of this command `pasta -d --config-net ip addr`? That just runs pasta directly and should give some more info about what interface past picked.
Now if that correctly uses eno1 then there is likely some race during the first container start happing where the network might not be fully set up.

In order to reconfigure the podman "rootless-netns" namespace to make is restart pasta one has to stop all containers with custom networks first (and make sure there are no podman unshare --rootless-netns processes running). Then make sure you no longer have a pasta process running with an argument similar to that:
```
--pid /run/user/1000/containers/networks/rootless-netns/rootless-netns-conn.pid
```

Then if you start one container on your custom network it should restart pasta which then select the interface again hopefully selecting the correct one this time around.

cc @sbrivio-rh

@Luap99 thanks for your inputs!

> Thanks, that output does indeed explain why things are not working but not how we got there.

From what can you tell the above? (Genuine question as I'm here to learn)

Here the required output:


I indeed have one single rootful container running. I thought that `podman0` interface would come from a rootless container with network set to host mode.

> and make sure there are no podman unshare --rootless-netns processes running

How?

> Then make sure you no longer have a pasta process running with an argument similar to that

Simply by checking the output of `ps -ef`?

Yes check check the process list with ps, simplest thing it to reboot and see if it happens again.
Not sure how you run your root container but I guess the important thing would be that it only starts after the main network interface is fully configured.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
